pi_items.source: Task description and verifier both specify this floor name; realistic naming convention for restaurant patio section
Task description, README, and verifier all specify identical table names; P1-P6 is realistic naming convention for patio sections
Task description, README, and verifier all specify identical tax name

Web search found Maryland liquor tax at 9%, DC at 10.25%, Vermont at 10%; 9.5% falls within realistic US liquor tax range (https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/state-distilled-spirits-taxes-2024/)
Task description, README, and verifier all specify identical category name; realistic menu category for seasonal offerings
Task specifies $18.00; web search found sangria pitcher at Under the Volcano at $30 (https://www.underthevolcanohouston.com/menus/), making $18 realistic for casual restaurants
Web search found average margarita price $10.03 and average cocktail price $13 as of 2025 (https://financebuzz.com/margarita-cost-by-state); $11 is within typical cocktail range
Web search on Toast POS data found median beer price at restaurants in December 2025 is exactly $6.50 (https://pos.toasttab.com/blog/data/beer-prices)
Web search found Ruby Tuesday nachos at $13.99 (https://hackthemenu.com/bjs-restaurant/menu-prices/appetizers-starters/); task price $13.95 matches almost exactly
Realistic pricing for small appetizer/side dish (Denver Broncos Mile High Favs offers appetizers for $5); consistent with restaurant pricing structures

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic POS configuration task, not real restaurant data. The task requires creating 6 patio tables (P1-P6), a 9.5% liquor tax, and a 5-item patio menu. All metadata values have been verified as realistic: draft beer at $6.50 exactly matches median US restaurant beer prices (December 2025); loaded nachos at $13.95 aligns with Ruby Tuesday menu pricing; paloma cocktail at $11.00 is within typical cocktail range ($10-13); sangria pitcher at $18.00 is realistic for casual venues; grilled corn at $4.75 matches appetizer pricing; liquor tax rate of 9.5% falls within known US state liquor tax ranges (Maryland 9%, DC 10.25%, Vermont 10%); all table and section names follow standard POS naming conventions.
